scenes.text: Every database has slow queries. Most teams find out from an angry user, or a two a.m. page. Sift finds them from the evidence, and fixes them safely. Let me show you, with two real developers and one D B R E.

I'm Dev. From my console I run a real, read-only query against our live Atlas collection. Phone orders, for older customers. And it's slow. A blocking, in-memory sort: a hundred thousand documents scanned, just to return fifteen. Sift captures the real explain plan and tags it to me.

I'm Aakash, and I run a completely different query. Seattle buyers, a different filter, a different sort. Slow in its own way. Two developers, two different problems, both captured automatically, with exactly who ran them.

Now I'm the D B R E. No guessing, no staring at dashboards. Every slow query my users actually ran, ranked by real explain evidence, over-scan and blocking sorts, and attributed to the person behind each one.

I diagnose the worst offender. Three Gemini Agent Engine roles, Diagnose, Candidate, and Rationale, run four read-only tools. They explain the plan, compare candidate indexes, and reason about the fix. Every one of them reads. Not one of them can write.

Because an agent can be wrong. So deterministic Python is the decision-maker. It selects the index by the Equality, Sort, Range rule, computes an evidence hash, and binds my approval to that exact hash. Nothing touches the database until I approve it.

So I approve. Watch what happens. The backend builds the index, and then re-runs the query against the live collection. A hundred thousand documents scanned, collapses to twenty-five. The blocking sort is gone. Verified. Not because the A I said so, but because we re-ran the real query and measured it.

And throughout, Sift Memory gives me read-only context. Voyage embeddings retrieve the right runbook guidance for this exact failure, ranked, scored, never a decision-maker. Real users, real evidence. A I that recommends, deterministic Python that decides, a human who approves, and proof that it worked. That's Sift.
